record.field: ezBookkeeping is a lightweight, self-hosted personal finance app that keeps full control of your bookkeeping data on your own hardware.

ezBookkeeping is a lightweight, self-hosted personal finance app that keeps full control of your bookkeeping data on your own hardware.

Built in Go and Vue.js, it lets you record daily transactions, import statements from dozens of formats, and analyze spending through rich charts and ad-hoc queries. It is exceptionally resource-efficient, deploys with a single Docker command, and runs anywhere from a Raspberry Pi to a clustered NAS while backing onto SQLite, MySQL or PostgreSQL.

Key features
  • Two-level accounts, categories and tags with scheduled transactions, attachments, tags and map-based location tracking
  • Multi-currency support with automatic exchange rates from 15+ central banks, plus 20+ languages including RTL
  • Broad import support: CSV, Excel, OFX, QFX, QIF, IIF, MT940, Camt, GnuCash, Firefly III, Beancount and more
  • Insights Explorer for advanced multi-condition queries with pie, line, treemap, sunburst and heatmap visualizations
  • Hardened auth: 2FA, OIDC and Nextcloud/Gitea/GitHub login, WebAuthn/PIN app lock and login rate limiting
  • Multi-user with per-account restrictions, PWA mobile/desktop UIs, dark mode and optional AI receipt recognition via MCP
Best for

Individuals and households who want a private, low-footprint ledger for personal finances, and homelab or NAS users seeking a self-hosted alternative to cloud budgeting tools without sacrificing analytics or import flexibility.

services: mysql: image: mysql:8.0 restart: unless-stopped environment: - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD={{ mysql_root_password }} - MYSQL_DATABASE={{ mysql_database }} - MYSQL_USER={{ mysql_user }} - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{ mysql_password }} volumes: - ./mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_mysql' networks: - internal ezbookkeeping: image: mayswind/ezbookkeeping restart: unless-stopped depends_on: - mysql environment: - EBK_SERVER_DOMAIN={{ ebk_server_domain }} - EBK_SERVER_ENABLE_GZIP={{ ebk_server_enable_gzip }} - EBK_DATABASE_TYPE={{ ebk_database_type }} - EBK_DATABASE_HOST={{ ebk_database_host }} - EBK_DATABASE_NAME={{ ebk_database_name }} - EBK_DATABASE_USER={{ ebk_database_user }} - EBK_DATABASE_PASSWD={{ ebk_database_passwd }} - EBK_LOG_MODE={{ ebk_log_mode }} - EBK_SECURITY_SECRET_KEY={{ ebk_security_secret_key }} volumes: - /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro - ./storage:/ezbookkeeping/storage - ./logs:/ezbookkeeping/log container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T mysql sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d mysql # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T mysql sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
